title: IPO bound Coal India arm CMPDIL garners Rs 470 crore from anchor investors
description: Central Mine Planning and Design Institute (CMPDIL) has raised Rs 470 crore from anchor investors, including LIC and several mutual funds, ahead of its Rs 1,842-crore IPO. The initial share sale, an offer for sale by Coal India, opens for public subscription on March 20 with a price band of Rs 163-Rs 172 per share.
one_line_insight: CMPDIL's successful anchor investment signals strong market confidence ahead of its IPO.

title: Crypto exchange Kraken freezes IPO plans, CoinDesk reports
description: Cryptocurrency exchange ​Kraken ​has put its multibillion-dollar ​initial public offering plan on hold, CoinDesk reported on ‌Wednesday, ⁠citing ⁠two people with knowledge ​of the matter
one_line_insight: Kraken's IPO freeze signals potential volatility in the fintech sector.

title: US Stocks: Microsoft considers legal action over $50 billion Amazon-OpenAI cloud deal: Report
description: Microsoft is considering legal action ​against its partner OpenAI and ​Amazon over a $50 billion deal that could violate its ​exclusive cloud agreement with the ChatGPT maker, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday.
one_line_insight: Legal disputes in the tech sector could signal volatility for major players.

title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
description: Indian markets rallied for a third day, led by auto, IT and financial stocks, while easing volatility supported sentiment. Technical indicators signal bullish momentum, with analysts projecting further upside amid global mixed cues and advising cautious, stock-specific strategies.
one_line_insight: Classification unavailable.

title: Market Trading Guide: Buy BSE and Atlanta Electricals on Thursday for gains up to 13%
description: Nifty rose sharply for a third session, driven by auto, IT and consumer stocks. Technical indicators signal bullish momentum, with analysts projecting upside towards 24,250 and support at 23,500, alongside breakout patterns strengthening sentiment.
one_line_insight: Classification unavailable.

title: BEW Engineering bulk deal: Ashish Kacholia exits SME company as stock slumps 44% in a year
description: Ace investor Ashish Kacholia exited BEW Engineering entirely through a bulk deal, amid prolonged stock underperformance and declining prices, raising concerns about the company’s near-term outlook despite its niche presence in pharmaceutical equipment manufacturing and past listing gains.
one_line_insight: The exit of a prominent investor from BEW Engineering signals significant concerns over its future performance.

title: US Federal Reserve expected to hold rates, weigh oil shock
description: Investors await Powell’s signals as the Federal Reserve weighs rates steady amid US-Israeli strikes on Iran, balancing persistent inflation against slowing growth risks, with new projections and policy language expected to reflect geopolitical uncertainty, labor market shifts, and rate cuts.
one_line_insight: Monitor geopolitical developments as they may influence market stability and investor sentiment.

title: US Stocks: Macy's forecasts weak 2026, says tariff hit to ease later this year
description: Macy's said ​it was taking a "prudent approach" to its outlook, as it ​forecast a fall in annual revenue and profit, citing macroeconomic and geopolitical risks that could affect consumer spending.
one_line_insight: Macy's cautious outlook signals potential challenges for consumer spending in the retail sector.

title: Claude Dispatch: Anthropic rolls out task delegation feature for Claude across devices in Cowork preview
description: Using the new feature, users can get summaries or reports based on data stored in local spreadsheets, search through Slack messages or email and generate briefing notes, and create formatted presentations using files stored in the cloud, among others.
one_line_insight: The new task delegation feature by Anthropic enhances productivity tools, signaling a positive shift in the IT sector.

title: Belgium's imec secures rare ASML High NA EUV tool to drive next-generation chips
description: Belgian chip research lab imec has acquired a state-of-the-art ASML High NA EUV lithography machine. This advanced tool will be central to imec's new pilot line.  Companies like Intel and SK Hynix plan to use similar machines for new AI chips by 2027.  This acquisition strengthens Europe's role in global semiconductor manufacturing.
one_line_insight: The acquisition of advanced lithography technology by imec positions Europe as a key player in the next generation of AI chip development.

title: BMG sues Anthropic for using Bruno Mars, Rolling Stones lyrics in AI training
description: Music company BMG Rights Management has sued AI startup Anthropic. The lawsuit claims Anthropic used copyrighted song lyrics to train its AI chatbots. This is one of many lawsuits against tech companies for using creative works. Anthropic previously settled a similar case with authors. The music industry is taking legal action over AI training data.
one_line_insight: The lawsuit against Anthropic highlights the growing legal challenges faced by AI startups in using copyrighted material.

title: Google to allow AI opt-out to ease UK competition concerns
description: Google is creating new search controls. Websites can now choose to avoid its generative AI features. This move addresses concerns from Britain's competition regulator about Google's search dominance. The company also plans simpler ways for users to change their default search engine. This aims to boost competition and user choice in the UK search market.
one_line_insight: Google's proactive measures to enhance competition could positively influence its market perception.

title: Karnataka taps Japan for Bengaluru smart city development
description: Karnataka plans to explore collaboration with Japan on smart city initiatives in Bengaluru. The proposed model will enable Japanese companies to partner with cities globally to design and implement solutions for urban challenges, backed by government support, according to a statement from the state’s IT/BT department.
one_line_insight: Karnataka's collaboration with Japan could enhance urban development and attract foreign investment.

title: X outage: Users report issues with app and website access
description: X outage: Downdetector reported that more than 3,836 outage reports were logged on Tuesday evening in India, with a spike in complaints around 8:24 PM.
one_line_insight: The significant outage reported could lead to user dissatisfaction and potential revenue impact for X.

title: Govt sets up strategic partnerships with global tech companies for IICT
description: India is boosting its creative technology sector. The government has partnered with global tech firms to support the Indian Institute of Creative Technologies. This initiative aims to enhance skills and foster innovation in animation, VFX, gaming, and more. The WAVES Bazaar platform will connect Indian creators with global markets.
one_line_insight: The government's strategic partnerships with global tech firms signal a strong push for innovation in India's creative technology sector.

title: Vedanta board to meet on this date to consider third interim dividend for FY26
description: Vedanta's board will meet on March 23 to consider the third interim dividend for 2025-26. The record date for shareholder entitlement is set for March 28, 2026. The trading window will close from March 19 to March 25, 2026.&nbsp;
one_line_insight: Vedanta's upcoming board meeting signals potential shareholder returns, enhancing investor confidence.

title: Micron Technology shares extend rally ahead of second quarter earnings
description: Micron Technology is projected to report a fiscal second quarter net income of $10.1 billion on $19.6 billion in revenue, according to the average of analyst estimates
one_line_insight: Micron's strong earnings forecast suggests a bullish outlook for the tech sector.

title: Brent crude rebounds 9.6% from day's low to $110 on reports of attack on Iran’s South Pars gas field
description: Crude oil prices rose 9% after an attack on Iran's South Pars gas field, part of the world's largest natural gas deposit. Reports suggest Israel, with US consent, targeted the site, escalating tensions and risking global energy security amid ongoing military conflict in the region.
one_line_insight: The escalation in Iran poses significant risks to energy prices and market stability.

title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold slides $171/oz; silver plunges $4.30 on hotter-than-expected US PPI
description: Gold and silver prices have sharply declined after a higher-than-expected US Producer Price Index and a rebound in crude oil prices. Gold fell to $4,837, while silver dropped to $76.63, marking significant monthly losses amid ongoing inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions.
one_line_insight: The sharp decline in gold and silver prices signals heightened inflation concerns and geopolitical tensions that could impact market stability.

title: Wall Street edges lower after producer prices data, Fed rate decision in focus
description: As of 12:55 p.m. Eastern Time, the S&amp;P 500 fell 0.6%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 0.8%, and the Nasdaq Composite was 0.6% lower.
one_line_insight: Market sentiment is bearish as Wall Street reacts to economic data and upcoming Fed decisions.

title: GSP Crop Science IPO: Issue subscribed 1.6 times on Day 3; NII segment sees strong demand, GMP signals flat listing
description: GSP Crop Science's IPO closed with a subscription rate of 1.64 times, driven by institutional interest. With a total issue size of  ₹400 crore, the company aims to use proceeds for debt repayment and operational purposes.
one_line_insight: Strong institutional demand for GSP Crop Science's IPO indicates positive market sentiment.

title: Central Mine Planning & Design Institute IPO: 5 key risks flagged in RHP that investors should know
description: Central Mine Planning &amp; Design Institute IPO is entirely an offer for sale (OFS) of 10.71 crore shares of face value of  ₹2 each. The price band for the issue has been set at  ₹163 to  ₹172 per share, which means the overall size of the OFS is about  ₹1,842.12 crore.
one_line_insight: Investors should be cautious of the highlighted risks in the upcoming IPO.

title: Investors should position for a longer Iran war. What to do now.
description: The risk is that the war lasts longer than expected, and that the shipping industry takes even longer to recover. The recent weakness in financial markets could be an amuse-bouche before a bearish feast.
one_line_insight: Prepare for potential market volatility as geopolitical tensions escalate.

title: Taking Stock: Market fails to hold on to day#39;s gains, ends marginally higher
description: Grasim Industries, Bharti Airtel, Nestle India, Maruti Suzuki and HCL Technologies were the biggest Nifty gainers, while losers included Sun Pharma, BPCL, Reliance Industries, MM and Hindalco Industries
one_line_insight: The market's inability to sustain gains suggests cautious investor sentiment.

title: Sensex, Nifty gain for third day in a row; easing volatility to support bull trend
description: Analysts expect the market to maintain the bullish undertone as elections and corporate earnings season continue
one_line_insight: The sustained gains in Sensex and Nifty indicate a bullish market trend, presenting potential investment opportunities.

title: Mid-day Mood | Cooling volatility sparks market rally, India VIX sees steepest fall in 4 years
description: Broader markets outperformed benchmarks, with the BSE Midcap and BSE Smallcap rising up to 0.9 percent
one_line_insight: The significant drop in India VIX indicates a favorable environment for market growth.

title: Sensex, Nifty extend gains to 3rd day, Q4 results to guide stock-specific action
description: India VIX, which measures near-term volatility saw a sharp fall of 18 percent to trade around 10.42
one_line_insight: The sustained gains in Sensex and Nifty indicate a bullish market sentiment ahead of Q4 results.
